# Cut & Move

> Cut & Move is a free macOS menu bar utility that adds real Cmd+X cut-and-move to Finder. It intercepts Cmd+X in Finder, enters cut mode, then converts the next Cmd+V into Cmd+Option+V (Finder's native "Move Item Here" command). Built by Microsoft MVP Richard Crane at MILL5.

## About

- **Name:** Cut & Move
- **Version:** 1.0
- **Platform:** MacOS 26.1 or later
- **Price:** Free
- **Stack:** Swift, SwiftUI, Cocoa — zero third-party dependencies
- **Author:** Richard Crane (Microsoft MVP, Founder of MILL5)
- **MVP profile:** https://mvp.microsoft.com/en-US/MVP/profile/10ce0bc0-7536-43f6-b28c-e9601a4a0d0d
- **MILL5:** https://mill5.com
- **Podcast:** *Inventing Fire with AI* — https://inventingfirewith.ai
- **Support:** rich@mill5.com

## How it works

1. Press **Cmd+X** with files selected in Finder. Cut & Move performs a silent Cmd+C and enters cut mode. The menu bar scissors icon fills in to confirm.
2. Navigate to the destination folder in Finder.
3. Press **Cmd+V**. Cut & Move injects the Option modifier so Finder receives Cmd+Option+V — the native *Move Item Here* command. The files are moved, not copied.
4. Press **Escape** or **Cmd+C** at any time to cancel cut mode.

## Why it exists

macOS Finder has never had a native Cmd+X keyboard shortcut for cut-and-move. The closest native equivalent is Cmd+C followed by Cmd+Option+V. Cut & Move fills the gap so the expected Cmd+X / Cmd+V flow just works, without replacing Finder or installing kernel extensions.

## Technical details

- Uses a `CGEvent` tap (`CFMachPort`) at the session level to intercept keyboard events
- Only processes events when Finder (`com.apple.finder`) is the frontmost application
- Tags simulated events with a `0xCAFE` magic number to prevent infinite loops
- Requires macOS Accessibility permissions for keyboard event monitoring
- App Sandbox must be disabled; Hardened Runtime is enabled
- No network access, no analytics, no telemetry, no data collection
